items.title: Four-week inquest into Kumanjayi White's death in custody set for April 2027

children.content: A four-week coronial inquest into the death of a 24-year-old man who died in a Coles supermarket in Alice Springs last year after being restrained by police officers has been set down for April 2027.

gallery.altText: A variety of flowers - white, yellow, plum, orange - laid on the ground at a memorial outside a supermarket.

thumbnail.altText: A variety of flowers - white, yellow, plum, orange - laid on the ground at a memorial outside a supermarket.

avatar.alt: A journalist wearing a button-up shirt, with a neutral facial expression.

items.title: Air quality monitoring to be upgraded in the NT after Inpex under-reporting

children.content: The Northern Territory government has announced a plan to introduce new air quality monitoring of toxic pollutants, including any carcinogenic emissions. It follows a review that found gas giant Inpex had underestimated its emissions in Darwin.

image.altText: A gas plant

items.title: Outbreak of this Victorian-era disease labelled a 'health disaster and tragedy'

children.content: Forty-two babies in Australia died from congenital syphilis between 2011 and 2025, with the outbreak driving health workers into communities to raise awareness of the old-world disease.

image.altText: A man inside a lab studying a human skull, a skeleton model hangs nearby.

items.title: Fair Work rejects gas giant's claim strikes would harm the economy

children.content: One of Australia's biggest gas producers has lost a bid to stop industrial action at its Darwin facilities after claiming strikes would force shutdowns and cause significant damage to the nation's economy.

image.altText: Evening / dusk shot of Darwin harbour, industrial site, with yellow lights glowing and cranes in view.

items.title: Police charge Tennant Creek teenager with 21-year-old's stabbing murder

children.content: Northern Territory Police charge a 17-year-old boy with the murder of a 21-year-old man who he allegedly stabbed to death in Tennant Creek last week.

image.altText: NT Police insignia on mud-splattered car door.

items.title: The one of a kind remote NT camp where 'it's cool to go to school'

children.content: Children from across Central Australia have joined a remote Northern Territory camp inside the Watarrka National Park where "it's cool to go to school" — and the footy doesn't hurt either.

gallery.altText: A child swings a baseball bat on a grassy, dirt field.

thumbnail.altText: A child swings a baseball bat on a grassy, dirt field.

images.altText: Vozinho celebrates by holding up a Cabo Verde flag

children.content: World Cup debutants Cabo Verde produce an incredible 90-minute performance to hold favourites Spain goalless, taking a point in Atlanta. Follow live.

items.title: Cabo Verde denies Spain in one of World Cup's biggest-ever upsets

updates.title: And off we go in Miami!

items.title: Sydney shark attack victim's arm amputated amid 'long road to recovery'

children.content: Leah Stewart was swimming at Coogee Beach when she was attacked just before 11am on Saturday.

image.altText: A blonde woman smiles, laying back with a small child in red on her chest

items.title: 'Highly migratory': Shark cull won't improve beach safety, expert says

children.content: Culling sharks would not reduce the risk of attacks, a marine biologist says, as the family of Sydney shark attack victim Leah Stewart says she has a "long road to recovery" ahead of her.

image.altText: A great white shark in the deep blue sea.

items.title: 'Never crossed my mind he wouldn't survive': Seven die daily from drug overdose

children.content: Australia recorded its deadliest year on record for drug overdoses, according to a new analysis, with an average of seven deaths per day.

gallery.altText: A woman sits on a park bench holding a photo of her and her son in a picture frame.

thumbnail.altText: A woman sits on a park bench holding a photo of her and her son in a picture frame.

items.title: Senator Duniam's resignation could open door for One Nation

children.content: Tasmanian senator Jonno Duniam's announcement that he intends to leave politics could be the leg up that One Nation needs.

image.altText: A woman speaks in front of a crowd.

items.title: Shadow treasurer says CGT inquiry 'running roughshod over democracy'

children.content: Shadow Treasurer Tim Wilson says the two-day Senate inquiry into the government's budget measures is "running roughshod over democracy". Follow live.

image.altText: Tim Wilson, wearing a blue tie and dark suit, looks to his right.

items.title: AFP begins inquiry into allegations Israeli forces assaulted flotilla activists

children.content: Australian Federal Police have begun inquiries into allegations that Israeli defence personnel sexually assaulted Australian activists on a flotilla headed to Gaza, following a meeting with Foreign Minister Penny Wong.

image.altText: Four women sit at a wooden bench, looking serious.

items.title: Police watchdog review imminent amid 'unfair public attack' accusation

children.content: The police watchdog is set to deliver its findings into an investigation of the response to Lindy Lucena's death after her family called for scrutiny.

image.altText: Lindy Lucena

items.title: 'Eye for an eye': How Captain Cook met his bloody end in Hawaii

children.content: Captain Cook set sail from Plymouth to find an elusive trade route linking Europe to Asia in 1776. He never saw England again.

gallery.altText: A coloured artwork depicting Captain Cook being stabbed by Hawaiians in the 1700s.
Captain Cook sits for a portrait while sat at a table on which sits his hat. He looks serious

thumbnail.altText: A coloured artwork depicting Captain Cook being stabbed by Hawaiians in the 1700s.
Captain Cook sits for a portrait while sat at a table on which sits his hat. He looks serious

items.title: Legendary band Australian Crawl reforms for first time in 40 years

children.content: Australian Crawl will lead a major line-up of classic Australian acts across a run of shows later this year.

image.altText: Australian Crawl members hold Sons of Beaches award plaques against a wood-paneled wall

items.title: With the success of 007 First Light, the future of James Bond is looking bright

children.content: While producers are still mulling over who will take the Bond mantle from Daniel Craig, the super-spy has already moved on in 007 First Light.

image.altText: A screenshot from 007 First Light

items.title: Algal bloom information 'inconsistent', amid survey of health impacts of the bloom

children.content: Volunteers surveyed more than 200 people who reported a range of symptoms during the algal bloom outbreak not represented in the SA government's health advice. A disability advocate says health information has been "inaccessible" and "inconsistent".

gallery.altText: A mother and son stand in front of an ocean looking at the camera

thumbnail.altText: A mother and son stand in front of an ocean looking at the camera

items.title: California governor accuses Trump of launching politically motivated investigation

children.content: Gavin Newsom, a longtime political rival of the Republican president, says federal agents have in recent days been knocking on the doors of his friends and former employees, asking for records.

image.altText: A man with dark grey hair wearing a navy shirt, standing in front of a white and red background

items.title: UK plans to ban social media for under-16s

children.content: Sir Keir Starmer says he will ban social media sites for under-16s and impose restrictions on gaming and live streaming platforms in some of the most far-reaching online restrictions globally.

image.altText: Keir Starmer wearing a black suit and teal tie, mid-speech while sitting at a wooden table and gesturing with his right hand.

items.title: McCullum goes from 'angry to gutted' after Stokes nightclub incident

children.content: England coach Brendon McCullum says he went from "bewildered to angry to gutted" after hearing captain Ben Stokes was involved in another late-night incident.

image.altText: Ben Stokes and Brendon McCullum converse in the England rooms during a game

items.title: Two men convicted of plot to torch property linked to British PM Keir Starmer

children.content: A Russian-speaking figure who went by the name "El Money" offered one of the men money to torch the properties and record video that could be posted online to draw attention to the attack.

image.altText: The mugshots of two young men side by side.

items.title: Hospital call from Melbourne's outer north leaders

children.content: A group of councils in Melbourne's north is calling on the state government to spend $45 million to buy land to begin planning for a hospital and train station.

gallery.altText: Man in blue blazer and white shirt standing next to man in navy jumper

thumbnail.altText: Man in blue blazer and white shirt standing next to man in navy jumper

items.title: Labor's CGT plan flawed but better than what we have, economists tell inquiry

children.content: Economists have offered qualified support for the government's plans on day one of a Senate inquiry.

image.altText: Albanese is in focus as he sits on the green bench watching Chalmers speak

items.title: PM to make fuel excise call early next week

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says the government will give Australians "appropriate notice" about the fuel excise rebate, as it continues to monitor the issue.

image.altText: A close up of Prime Minister Anthony Albanese in a white hard hat and a yellow high-vis vest.

items.title: Cairns man charged after allegedly naming man at centre of extortion case

children.content: A Cairns man accused of exposing the identity of a Queensland public figure at the centre of a mysterious extortion case has been charged by police with allegedly disobeying a suppression order.

image.altText: A designed image with the Cairns Courthouse and a silhouette of a man on red background.

items.title: Private schoolboy plotted attack on LNP, court hears

children.content: Prosecutor says a private schoolboy who allegedly planned to bomb a public event was inspired by the "Unabomber".

image.altText: A black sign showing Brisbane's Supreme and District Court

items.title: Are Australia's environment laws still 'broken'?

children.content: The clearing of koala habitat at a Brisbane school has enraged advocates and raised questions about the federal government's sweeping changes to national environment laws.

items.title: 'Sharp increase' in drone strikes leads to 1,000 civilian deaths in Sudan

children.content: The United Nations condemns an increase in drone attacks in Sudan, where four years of war has created the world's largest displacement and hunger crises.

image.altText: Four men, wearing white robes, stand, looking at a mass grave in the dirt, covered with branches.

items.title: Heritage-listed Ukrainian monastery severely damaged by Russian strike

children.content: Four people are dead after a Russian strike hit a 900-year-old monastery in central Kyiv.

image.altText: An aerial shot of a golden domed monastry with fire trucks putting out a fire.

items.title: White House UFC event ends with massive upset win in title fight

children.content: US President Donald Trump watches ring-side as the UFC event dubbed Freedom 250 ends with Justin Gaethje scoring a massive upset win over Ilia Topuria at the White House.

gallery.altText: Justin Gaethje shakes Donald Trump's hand.
A bloodied Ilia Topuria hangs his head as he is carried away from the octagon at UFC Freedom 250.
Diego Lopes stands on top of the octagon at the White House.

thumbnail.altText: Justin Gaethje shakes Donald Trump's hand.
A bloodied Ilia Topuria hangs his head as he is carried away from the octagon at UFC Freedom 250.
Diego Lopes stands on top of the octagon at the White House.
